stimulus: Streaming services restrict access to certain shows to those with a paid subscription. In an investigation of the effect of launching a streaming service on cable network revenues for subscriptions and advertising, Devon Park and colleagues compared actual outcomes (with a streaming service) to control estimates (without a streaming service). The researchers concluded that launching a streaming service is generally more beneficial for larger cable networks, which have wider audiences and tend to own substantial original programming.
stem: Which choice best describes data from the table that support Park and colleagues' conclusion?

answerOptions.content: Apex Sports had an 18.4% revenue change, while Highland Local News had a −2% revenue change.
Highland Local News had only a −2% revenue change, which was the smallest percentage change of the selected networks.
The Metro Family Network and Coastal Movies had similar total revenue changes, but Coastal Movies had a smaller percentage change.
Coastal Movies had an 11.2% revenue change, while Apex Sports had an 18.4% revenue change.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The conclusion is that streaming services benefit larger networks more. Comparing the large network Apex Sports (a revenue gain of 18.4%) with the small network Highland Local News (a revenue loss of 2%) directly supports that conclusion.

Choice B is incorrect; it discusses only a small network. Choice C is incorrect; it compares two large networks with no small-network comparison. Choice D is incorrect; it compares two large networks with no small-network comparison.
